nameMeanings.name: Preaux
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Jean-Pierre.

French (Gréaux): metathesized form of Géraud, a variant of Gérald (see Gerald ). This surname is very rare in France; it is found mainly in the West Indies (mainly Saint Barthélemy, Guadeloupe, and United States Virgin Islands).

variants.name: Gerald
meanings: English (of Norman origin); French (Gérald): from the ancient Germanic personal name Gerald, Girald, composed of the elements gēr ‘spear, lance’ and wald ‘power, authority’. It was introduced to Britain by the Normans and was fairly common, but less so than Gerard , with which it was early confused. In France the much more common variants of this surname are Géraud and Giraud .

nameMeanings.name: Albreda
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Variant of   Elfreda  , but now probably taken as a feminine equivalent of   Alfred .

en.lifeSummary: Albreda Preaux was born in 1007, in Normandy, France. She had at least 5 sons and 3 daughters with Hubert De Ryes Steward Of Normandy. She died in 1112, in Somme, Picardie, France, at the age of 105.
